title: calycobolus campanulatus subsp. campanulatus
intro: Calycobolus campanulatus subsp. campanulatus, also known as the Bellflower Vine or Calycobolus, is a type of flowering vine that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Calycobolus campanulatus subsp. campanulatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, or using it in combination with other herbs to create a medicinal tincture.
